LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE JAN-02-2024 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO GIANTS ENTERPRISES LLC'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. MATTER IS OFF CALENDAR. THE DECEMBER 5, 2023, FRASSETTO DECLARATION FAILS TO SHOW THE PARTIES MET AND CONFERRED AS REQUIRED BY CCP 430.31. COUNSEL ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER IMMEDIATELY. THE RESPONSE TO THE AMENDED COMPLAINT IS NOW DUE BY FEBRUARY 2, 2024. JUDGE: JOSEPH M. QUINN; CLERK: S. KANE; NOT REPORTED (302/JMQ)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-08-2024 Continued To Sep-25-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-03-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO GIANTS ENTERPRISES LLC (ERRONEOUSLY SUED HEREIN AS "SAN FRANCISCO GIANTS ENTERPRISES, LLC") DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

Defendant San Francisco Giants Enterprises LLC's ("SFGE") demurrer to the second amended complaint is sustained with leave to amend.

Plaintiff Jeffrey Perlow alleges he was playing bubble soccer on SFGE's premises and was injured due to SFGE's failure to inspect ground/floors/surfaces, failure to train participants and staff, and service of alcohol. "Ordinarily, negligence may be alleged in general terms without specific facts showing how the injury occurred, but there are limits to the generality with which a plaintiff is permitted to state his cause of action" (Berkeley v. Downs (2007) 152 Cal.App.4th 518, 527 [internal quotes omitted].) Once again, Perlow fails to meet this lenient pleading standard.

Negligence requires showing duty, breach, causation, and damages. Perlow's negligence claim fails to set forth at least the third element. Perlow alleges SFGE's breach of various duties (e.g., failure to inspect ground/floors/surfaces, failure to train participants and staff, service of alcohol) caused his injuries. How? SFGE is left to speculate whether Perlow tripped over an uneven floorboard, was knocked over by an inebriated participant or untrained staff member, or infinite other possibilities. (See Potter v. Richards (1955) 132 Cal.App.2d 380, 384-385 [allegations hospital was understaffed and so caused patient's death failed to show proximate cause].) Unlike the unconscious surgery patient in Guilliams v. Hollywood Hospital (1941) 18 Cal.2d 97, Perlow pleads no facts to suggest he was unaware of what happened. Perlow likewise fails to allege the proximate cause element of his premises liability claim.

SFGE's demurrer is sustained with leave to amend and file another red-lined complaint.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-03-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO GIANTS ENTERPRISES LLC's MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT.

Defendant San Francisco Giants Enterprises LLC's ("SFGE") motion to strike the second amended complaint ("SAC") is denied.

CCP 436(b) allows a court, in its discretion, to strike out a pleading not filed in conformity with the law or court rules. SFGE argues the court should strike plaintiff Jeffrey Perlow's SAC because his attorney failed to adhere to the ten-day filing deadline under CRC 3.1320. This motion is largely rendered moot by the court's ruling on the companion demurrer. In any event, the court declines to strike a pleading based on a mere 18-day delay where no prejudice to SFGE has been shown.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling in complainve with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-17-2024 Continued To Jul-02-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-08-2024 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO GIANTS ENTERPRISES LLC's DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

San Francisco Giants Enterprises LLC's demurrer to the first amended complaint (FAC) is sustained with leave to amend.

"Ordinarily, negligence may be alleged in general terms, without specific facts showing how the injury occurred, but there are limits to the generality with which a plaintiff is permitted to state his cause of action" (Berkley v. Downs (2007) 152 Cal.App.4th 518, 527 [internal quote marks omitted].) This FAC exceeds those limits.

Plaintiffs' own statement of their case makes the Berkley point. The Giants are said to have "operated the bubble soccer game in a negligent manner." How? The Giants are said to have "failed to remedy a dangerous condition of the property." What condition? The Giants are said to have "negligently maintained the premises" In what way(s)? (See Opp. 2:3-9.) The
FAC answers none of these key questions.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported.(302/RBU)
